program.name: <program-redacted> (VDP)
program.authorization_reference: Public <bug-bounty-platform> VDP policy page metadata reviewed on 2026-05-25 via https://<bug-bounty-platform>.com/<program-slug>?type=team. Target-touching work remains blocked until selected assets are explicitly mirrored into config/scope.txt and the operator approves the single-lane dry-run packet.

in_scope.notes: Policy explicitly says to use this path for new account signup. Candidate first-lane asset; do not navigate until operator confirms adding exact host/path to config/scope.txt.
Policy explicitly says to use this path for research login. Candidate first-lane asset; do not navigate until operator confirms adding exact host/path to config/scope.txt.
Derived only from explicit research signup/login URLs; selected solely for auth/session/account-entry lane, pending operator confirmation.

reference_only.notes: Policy references product documentation; reference-only for understanding intended use, not a selected test target.
Policy references API documentation; reference-only for understanding intended use, not a selected test target.
Policy references run-script documentation; reference-only. Run-script testing is blocked for first lane.

out_of_scope.reason: Policy lists HTTPS / TLS security headers suggestions out of scope.
Policy lists direct testing of third parties out of scope.
Policy lists SPF / DMARC / DKIM / DNSSEC suggestions out of scope.
Policy lists banner/version disclosure out of scope.
Policy lists social engineering / phishing / spam out of scope.
Policy says do not disrupt services (DoS/DDoS).
Policy says do not access, modify, or destroy accounts/data that do not belong to you.
Policy says do not disclose vulnerabilities outside the VDP.

conditionally_allowed_later: api_checks_only_after policy and documentation review plus operator approval
techniques.automation_notes: First lane remains manual/noVNC/browser-assisted only. The policy mentions Burp/ZAP header configuration, but no automated scanner/fuzzer/DAST permission was observed.

session_layer.required_account_identity: Use <bug-bounty-platform> email alias <researcher-alias-email> OR add X-<bug-bounty-platform>-Research: [H1 username] header when testing.
session_layer.header_notes: Browser-only steps may not set custom headers unless using a proxy. If not using the <bug-bounty-platform> alias, configure the header in Burp/ZAP before target-touching requests. Do not store the operator's real email, alias, or cookies in repo artifacts.
session_layer.account_rule: Only interact with accounts owned by the operator or explicitly permitted by the account holder. Operator handles CAPTCHA/OTP/email verification locally.

rate_limits.notes: Conservative project default; no explicit numeric rate limit observed in policy intake. Stop on CAPTCHA, account warning, rate limiting, bot detection, or policy ambiguity.

first_lane.name: researcher_account_auth_session_profile_workspace_empty_state_surface_map
first_lane.goal: Complete legal workflow practice with minimal owned-account auth/session/profile/empty-state mapping; expected status no_finding or surface_only unless clear owned-account impact appears.

reporting_rules.impact_focus: Policy asks researchers to consider real-world impact such as privilege escalation, sensitive information disclosure, or ability to affect resources not owned by the tenant/team.

expiration.notes: Re-check <bug-bounty-platform> policy before target-touching work after this date or if <bug-bounty-platform> shows updates.
